title: Pinalia atrovinosa
intro: Pinalia atrovinosa, also known as the black-flowered Pinalia, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Pinalia atrovinosa has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Pinalia atrovinosa is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea form, or used in the production of tonics and extracts to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Pinalia atrovinosa is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Pinalia. This genus is part of the family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, Pinalia atrovinosa is placed in the subfamily Epidendroideae. Further classification within the subfamily places it in the tribe Malaxideae. Pinalia atrovinosa is also classified as a type of terrestrial orchid, native to Southeast Asia.
